steps: Profile the raw dataset: row count, null rates, type distributions, value ranges
Define expected schema: types, nullability, value constraints per column
Handle missing values: drop, impute, or flag — decide per column, document reasoning
Standardise formats: dates to ISO 8601, strings stripped and lowercased if appropriate
Deduplicate: define the primary key, resolve conflicts by recency or confidence score
Validate against the schema and reject or quarantine non-conforming rows
Log a cleaning report: rows in, rows out, rows quarantined, changes per column
Write idempotent pipeline — running twice must produce identical output
constraints: never modify raw data in place — always write to a new output
quarantine bad rows — do not silently drop them
failure_modes: silently dropping rows that fail validation, hiding data quality issues
imputing missing values without documenting the assumption
non-idempotent deduplication producing different row counts on re-runs
treating encoding errors as empty strings instead of flagging them
